mechanics.name: Warp
mechanics.summary: An alternative cost: cast the card from hand for its warp cost, exile it at the beginning of the next end step, then cast it later from exile for its full cost. Cheap tempo and ETB value now, a real threat later — and every warped spell switches on Void that turn.

mechanics.name: Station
mechanics.summary: Tap another creature you control (sorcery-speed) to put charge counters equal to its power on a Spacecraft or Planet; hitting printed thresholds unlocks abilities and usually turns the Spacecraft into an attacking creature. Wants expendable bodies and rewards going wide — 22 Spacecraft in the set.

mechanics.name: Void
mechanics.summary: Ability word: a bonus if a nonland permanent left the battlefield this turn or a spell was warped this turn. Fed by warp, sacrifice, removal, and bounce — the blue/black axis of the set.

mechanics.name: Landfall + Landers
mechanics.summary: Classic landfall triggers plus Lander artifact tokens ('{2}, {T}, Sacrifice: search a basic, put it in tapped'); ~20 cards make or use Landers, letting any deck bank instant-speed landfall triggers.

mechanics.name: Surveil
mechanics.summary: A small blue/black graveyard-smoothing theme; several payoffs care about cards entering the graveyard.

mechanics.name: Planets
mechanics.summary: Five legendary Planet lands that can themselves be stationed to unlock powerful effects; free spell-lands for the aggressive decks and mana sinks for the slow ones.

archetypes.name: Second-spell aggro
archetypes.playstyle: Curve out and chain cheap spells so you double-spell from turn three onward; Station Monitor and Cosmogrand Zenith convert every second spell into flying Drones, Soldier tokens, and +1/+1 counters while one-mana interaction and cantrips keep the count alive.

archetypes.name: Token go-wide attrition
archetypes.playstyle: Flood the board with Soldier and Robot tokens and creatures that leave a body behind, then turn the pile lethal with Syr Vondam's deathtouch pump and death-trigger drain. Token makers are thin at common, so a clean curve plus black's removal does most of the actual winning.

archetypes.name: Station aggro
archetypes.playstyle: Flood the board with cheap bodies and Robot tokens, attack relentlessly, and tap spare creatures to station Spacecraft that finish the job as fliers; end-step triggers then pay you off for the tapped team. Curve out and stay tapped — the deck rewards committing, not holding back.

archetypes.name: +1/+1 counters aggro
archetypes.playstyle: Lead with above-rate two- and three-drops, then stack +1/+1 counters until they outclass every blocker and keep attacking; Haliya converts counter-carrier damage into cards, so force bad blocks and punish them with tricks.

archetypes.name: Void control
archetypes.playstyle: Churn cheap artifacts through sacrifice, bounce, and warp to keep Void switched on, then answer everything with upgraded Void removal while out-drawing the opponent; a sweeper or stationed Spacecraft closes the grind.

archetypes.name: Artifact aggro
archetypes.playstyle: Flood the board with cheap artifacts and Robot/Drone tokens so every entry grows a threat through Mm'menon and pumps Warmaker Gunship's artifact count; curve low, double-spell early, and convert the tempo before slower decks stabilize.

archetypes.name: Lander ramp
archetypes.playstyle: Ramp with Lander tokens and cheap fixers into an oversized top end, holding Landers to crack at instant speed for landfall blowouts with Genemorph Imago and Glacier Godmaw. Skew the curve high — the deck wins the late game it inevitably reaches.

archetypes.name: Void sacrifice
archetypes.playstyle: Trade, sacrifice, and warp something every turn so Void never switches off, upgrading Plasma Bolt and Tragic Trajectory into premium removal while Elegy Acolyte and Interceptor Mechan grind the card advantage. Cheap expendable bodies plus sac outlets are the engine; the opponent runs out of creatures and cards first.

archetypes.name: Graveyard value
archetypes.playstyle: Green ramp bodies plus black removal, with Broodtender and Fell Gravship stocking the yard to rebuy your best creature or Spacecraft. The recursion is a value layer on a good-stuff midrange shell, not a dedicated engine — win the long game on card quality.

archetypes.name: Lander landfall
archetypes.playstyle: Bank Lander tokens early, then crack them at instant speed for second land drops that double-trigger landfall — Tannuk pings into card draw, Harmonizer doubles power, Godmaw alpha-strikes. Ramp into oversized trampers while efficient red removal clears the road.

combos.note: a {U} warp cast is the cheapest possible second spell for Monitor's Drone trigger, and the later exile recast triggers it again
Vocalist doubles the Hound's ETB dig, and the warp recast from exile serves up the doubled trigger a second time
one Boosters cast is two permanents entering (Equipment plus Robot token), covering most of Haliya's three-life end-step draw
Zealot's free instant-speed creature sacrifice converts spare bodies into Vondam counters and lifegain while surveilling
sorcery-speed stationing keeps two creatures tapped so Chaplain's end-step counter always fires while the Sweeper charges to its 4+ flier
Turbodrone's sorcery-speed self-tap switches on Sami's two-tapped-creatures check, and the tapped Robot she mints keeps it on
Sunborn's token doubling turns Zenith's second-spell mode into four Soldiers per trigger
Loading Zone doubles Ouroboroid's power-equal counter dump on every creature, tripling its own power each combat
Culler's recastable graveyard warp switches on Elegy's Void every single turn for an end-step 2/2 Robot
tapping Sentinel to station untaps another creature so it can station the Labship again the same turn
cracking Relic at instant speed draws a card and switches on Actuator's enters-Void flashback of a cheap spell
Emissary's cast-trigger Drone is itself an artifact entering, chaining into Assembler's once-a-turn 2/2 Robot
Engineer's Lander cracks at instant speed to fire Imago's landfall for a surprise 3/3-or-6/6 mid-combat
every tap of Agrarian to station the Scanship mints a Lander whose crack feeds his landfall counters, raising future charge
a pre-combat Bolt marks the target with damage so Faithful's ETB kill gives the opponent no consolation cards
an instant-speed five charge counters flips the Sawship past its 3+ threshold into a surprise flying haste attacker
cracking Wayfarer's Lander mid-combat fires Harmonizer's landfall to double an attacker's power at instant speed
Scoutlander's sac-a-Lander double basic fetch resolves Tannuk's landfall twice in one shot, upgrading it into the draw
Scrapper's repeatable artifact sacrifice draws cards while machine-gunning the Specialist's 2-damage pings
Full Bore's warp-cast bonus gives the turn-3 warped Colossus haste and trample so its one warped turn actually attacks
